and if/then if (connects to previous statement)
a heavy rain (that hits the garden)
and for him/it (referring to the garden mentioned before)
and then it struck it (the garden)
and then it (the garden) burned up (after the whirlwind with fire hit it)
like that (referring to what was just mentioned)
perhaps you all/so that you all
you all spend! (command to a group)
you all close eyes/overlook (in relation to accepting poor quality charity)
forgiveness (as the thing being promised by Allah)
the wisdom (as the thing being given)
you (all) spent (referring to what was spent in charity)
any vow or pledge (being acted upon)
then indeed (connects to previous statement about spending)
and whatever (referring to whatever you spend)
if (introduces the condition 'if you disclose charity' in this ayah)
you all hide it (referring to charity)
the people who lack what they need (as the ones receiving the charity)
your (all of you) evil deeds (that Allah will remove)
you (all) work/do (referring to what Allah knows about)
except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
you all spend/give away (in conditional form)
it will be paid in full
for the poor people (who are mentioned in the rest of the verse)
they were restricted (referring to the poor people mentioned before)
traveling/moving about (as the action being done)
rich ones (plural) (those who the ignorant person thinks are wealthy)
you recognize them (referring to the poor believers mentioned earlier)
by their mark (you can recognize them by this)
persistent pressing/urging (describing how they don't ask people)
they stand (referring to those who consume usury)
has passed (referring to previous dealings with interest)
so those (referring to those who return to usury)
they (referring to those who consume usury)
indeed/truly (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
their Lord (referring to the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
they become/will become sad (referring to the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
then be aware/take notice (all of you)
you all turned back (in response to Allah's command)
you (all) wrong/oppress (in this ayah, addressing those who lend with interest)
and if (connects to previous rules about dealing with debts)
and protect yourselves! (command to a group)
you all dealt with each other in debt
between you all (in the context of mutual transactions)
with/by justice (describing how something should be done)
just as (referring to how Allah taught)
upon him (the one who owes the debt)
he decreases/shortchanges (in context of not shortchanging anything from a debt)
from him/it (referring to not diminishing anything from the debt)
then let him dictate (the one who has the obligation)
and if, then if (connects to previous statement)
she goes astray (in context: lest one [of the two women] should go astray)
one of them both (referring to the two women witnesses)
small (describing what is being written down)
big/large (describing what is being written about)
more/most just (than what was written before)
you all record/write down it (referring to the debt/transaction)
you all made deals with each other
a writer/scribe (the one writing the contract)
then if (connects to previous statement about not finding a scribe)
the one who (is trusted with something)
his trust (the thing entrusted to him)
you (all) do/work (referring to what Allah knows about your actions)
your selves (referring to what is within you in this ayah)
He will take account of you all
and so He forgives (referring to Allah)
you carry/bear (negative command: 'do not burden us')
you carried it/him (referring to the burden mentioned in the ayah)
those who (referring to people mentioned before)
O our Lord! (calling out to Allah)
burden us (as a request to Allah)
with/by it (referring to what we cannot bear)
and pardon! (as a command to Allah)
